scope: ACL 2027 supplement claim-boundary examples visual iteration

motivation.target_page_before: page 5 was a low-density overview page containing only the 'What This Supplement Does Not Prove' text block
motivation.goal_alignment: Add more registered render evidence and reduce the sparse text-only appearance without changing the evidence boundary.

local_visual_review.notes: The rendered page now has a clear render-first claim-boundary strip below the overview text. The figure is not clipped, captions do not overlap, and the rows connect real render evidence to bounded claims. Residual risk: some in-figure text remains small, but the page is substantially less sparse than the original text-only page.

verification.targeted_red: python -m pytest -q tests/test_paper_layout.py -k "claim_boundary_examples" failed before implementation: 2 failed, 49 deselected.
verification.targeted_green: python -m pytest -q tests/test_paper_layout.py -k "claim_boundary_examples" passed after implementation: 2 passed, 49 deselected.
verification.build: make -C paper acl27-supplement passed and produced a 42-page A4 supplement.
verification.pdf_text_scan: pdftotext found the new Figure S2 caption and did not find old red-material caption tokens, fig_vlm_grounding_cases, author-identifying tokens, or local path tokens in the PDF text scan.

claim_boundary.paper_use: visual reading guide in the overview section
claim_boundary.notes: The figure reuses tracked render panels and does not introduce a new metric, VLM run, material experiment, or navigation run.
